claim: Oasis and Purification II are sustained CPU-dominant in live combat.
support: CPU/GPU utilization lead, 93.9–95.5% CPU-dominant samples, 12.89–13.40 ms active GT and much lower frozen active GT.
boundary: Utilization is ~1 Hz and does not identify the owner of each individual frame.

claim: Live world/actor simulation is the primary added cost.
support: Frozen controls explicitly disable AI and pause/dilate the world; active GT falls by 7.79–8.45 ms while static rendering remains present.
boundary: The control changes several simulation dimensions together, so it is not a single-variable AI-only experiment.

claim: Actor/component ticks are the largest actionable named subcategory.
support: Published inclusive timer is ~4.1 ms in both cases versus roughly 1.0–1.2 ms/frame in frozen controls.
boundary: Inclusive timer families overlap; they must not be summed or treated as exclusive percentages.

claim: A specific AI class, Blueprint, GC event or physics task is the exact root owner.
support: No owner-resolved live call tree is embedded in the artifact.
boundary: Requires the P0 focused capture; the report deliberately does not invent a class name.

claim: The 200–330 ms hitches are streaming, GC, physics or PSO stalls.
support: The report UI labels are threshold-based heuristics.
boundary: Requires timestamp-aligned trace channels and cold/warm separation.

claim: Kill count alone explains active CPU time.
support: Purification II has 23 kills and Oasis 91 yet nearly identical CPU pressure; Moon has only 10 kills and 13.05 ms active GT.
boundary: Thirteen level aggregates are confounded and too few for causal regression.
